title: Growth model interpretation of planet size distribution
description: Figure 1 from Zeng et al., PNAS 2019. Mass-radius scatter plot of exoplanets with theoretical composition lines, temperature color encoding, planet classification regions, and radius distribution histogram.

L2_specific: mass-radius scatter plot classifies exoplanets into rocky, water, transitional, and gas giant regions
theoretical composition lines show density and material boundaries
temperature color gradient encodes stellar flux and equilibrium temperature
radius histogram reveals bimodal distribution in planet sizes
L3_detailed: growth model framework interprets the observed bimodal planet size distribution by mapping exoplanet mass and radius against theoretical composition curves for Earth-like rocky, water-rich, and H2/He-envelope planets
multi-layer visualization integrates scatter data, composition models, atmospheric escape boundaries, and solar system reference points to classify the exoplanet population
